system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes

    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Both errors persist from all previous attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Various blocks are "inside-out" or have "inward-pointing faces"
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Root Cause

After 6 attempts, the fundamental problem is that the **vertex positions combined with the block connectivity create geometrically invalid hexahedral blocks**. The blocks either collapse into degenerate shapes or have incorrect winding. Previous attempts to fix vertex ordering haven't resolved this because the underlying topology itself is problematic.

## DIFFERENT APPROACH — Minimal Viable Mesh with Proper Topology

The key insight is that the **block topology must be physically realizable**. Instead of trying to force the complex multi-stem geometry into valid hexahedra, I propose a **different vertex layout and block structure** that maintains the square-bend concept while ensuring mathematically valid blocks.

### 1. Redefine Vertices — Create Proper Intermediate Planes

Introduce intermediate vertices to break the domain into valid 3D-extending blocks:

```
vertices
(
    // Inlet face (x = -50)
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    // Plane at x = 0 (inner corner region)
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Plane at x = 25 (outer bend exit)
    ( 25 -25  25)   // 8
    ( 25 -25 -25)   // 9

    // Plane at x = -100 (stem start)
    (-100 -25  25)   // 10
    (-100 -25 -25)   // 11

    // Plane at x = -200
    (-200 -25  25)   // 12
    (-200 -25 -25)   // 13

    // Plane at x = -300
    (-300 -25  25)   // 14
    (-300 -25 -25)   // 15

    // Plane at x = -400
    (-400 -25  25)   // 16
    (-400 -25 -25)   // 17

    // Outlet plane (x = -500)
    (-500 -25  25)   // 18
    (-500 -25 -25)   // 19
);
```

### 2. Define 6 Valid Blocks Following Right-Hand Rule

```
blocks
(
    // Inlet to inner-corner (x = -50 to x = 0)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Inner-corner to bend-exit (x = 0 to x = 25)
    hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 11 10)       (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Bend-exit to stem-start (x = 25 to x = -100)
    hex (8 9 11 10 7 6 13 12)     (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Stem segment 1 (x = -100 to x = -200)
    hex (10 11 13 12 14 15 17 16) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Stem segment 2 (x = -200 to x = -300)
    hex (12 13 15 14 16 17 19 18) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Stem segment 3 (x = -300 to x = -400)
    hex (14 15 17 16 18 19 21 20) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Stem segment 4 (x = -400 to x = -500)
    hex (16 17 19 18 20 21 23 22) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

**Critical fix**: Ensure every block follows the right-hand rule. For each `hex (v0 v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6 v7)`:
- Vertices `v0,v1,v2,v3` form one face
- Vertices `v4,v5,v6,v7` form the opposite face
- Both faces wind CCW when viewed from outside the block

### 3. Update Boundary Conditions to Match New Vertex Indices

Rewrite all boundary face definitions to reference the correct vertex indices from the new vertex layout. The boundary patches should include:
- `inlet`: face (0 3 2 1)
- `outlet`: face (18 19 21 20) — but verify this connects to the last block
- Wall boundaries connecting the various wall faces

### 4. Alternative — Single Straight Section Plus Bend (Simpler)

If the multi-stem approach proves difficult, use a **simpler 3-block topology** that captures the essence of the square bend:

```
vertices
(
    (-50  25  25)   // 0    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2    (-50 -25  25)   // 3
    (  0  25  25)   // 4    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6    (  0 -25  25)   // 7
    ( 25 -25  25)   // 8    ( 25 -25 -25)   // 9
    (-100 -25  25)   // 10   (-100 -25 -25)   // 11
    (-200 -25  25)   // 12   (-200 -25 -25)   // 13
    (-300 -25  25)   // 14   (-300 -25 -25)   // 15
    (-400 -25  25)   // 16   (-400 -25 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -25  25)   // 18   (-500 -25 -25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)    // inlet section
    hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 11 10)       (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)    // bend section
    hex (8 9 11 10 12 13 15 14)    (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)    // stem section
    hex (12 13 15 14 16 17 19 18)  (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)    // stem extension
    hex (16 17 19 18 20 21 23 22)  (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)    // stem to outlet
);
```

The key difference from previous attempts: **Block 2** `hex (8 9 11 10 12 13 15 14)` reverses the winding compared to earlier attempts to ensure the right-hand rule is satisfied. The face 8→9→11→10 and face 12→13→15→14 must both wind CCW when viewed from outside.

## Summary

The persistent errors stem from the fact that the original vertex positions and block connectivity create geometrically invalid hexahedra. The solution requires restructuring the vertex layout to introduce proper intermediate planes and defining blocks that each span a valid 3D region. The critical fix is ensuring every block follows the right-hand rule with consistent CCW winding of both faces. Start with the simpler 3-block or 5-block approach above, verify the winding is correct, and update boundary faces accordingly.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
